## **Hello, geeks!**

The World Cup has landed in the US, and the platforms are in an all-out brawl to own where fans gather, react and share it.
-

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/71414d6f-9523-4742-b387-51e17e48ccc4/image.png?t=1781261284)
Caption: [Image: Meta]

-
Facebook's bolted on a ["Football Mode"](https://www.threads.com/@mattnavarra/post/DZdH6ERkXAv). 

Instagram's got a "Goal!" voice effect that [detonates an animation in your DMs](https://www.digitaltrends.com/social-media/instagrams-voice-message-effects-now-let-you-sound-like-a-pirate-a-grandma-or-a-world-cup-fan/). 

Threads has built a whole [World Cup hub](https://www.threads.com/@mattnavarra/post/DZdIDLViqLQ) — live scores, football stickers, legend-hosted Live Chats.

If it's got a logo, it's had a football glow-up. Resistance is futile.

Also this week: Instagram is _finally_ letting you [reorder your grid](https://www.threads.com/@mattnavarra/post/DZVOFqlipGW) — a decade late, but who's counting (thanks Instagram for the early access).
-

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/8e70ecdd-cdb1-4c9a-9f18-0e4bcf7c5bfe/image.png?t=1781261347)
Follow image link: (https://www.threads.com/@mattnavarra/post/DZVOFqlipGW)
Caption: 

-
LinkedIn's built a [creator marketplace](https://news.linkedin.com/2026/how-b2b-brands-can-drive-impact-with-creators-and-stronger-creat) much like Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube’.  

It’s a land grab for the B2B creator budgets agencies have been brokering off-platform, now routed through its own ad tools so it takes a cut. 

TikTok's testing [in-app audio calls](https://www.threads.com/@mattnavarra/post/DZcHMIwCjHB) — a late scramble to catch Snap and Instagram, who built messaging into their core. 
-

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/29ab6fbd-2cdb-4803-9f53-b44852bba32e/image.png?t=1781261416)
Follow image link: (https://www.threads.com/@mattnavarra/post/DZcHMIwCjHB)
Caption: [Image: @jonahmanzano]

-
As DMs quietly become where social actually happens, TikTok's playing catch-up.
-
Meanwhile, the teen social media ban is the must-have government policy of 2026. 

Australia's got one. Canada's [joining in](https://www.reuters.com/legal/litigation/canada-introduces-legislation-ban-social-media-children-under-16-2026-06-10/). Now British PM Sir Keir Starmer's is reportedly [dropping in his next week](https://www.politico.eu/article/ministers-to-ban-under-16s-from-social-media-livestreaming-disappearing-messages/).

A cracking win against a group of people too young to vote. Whether it keeps a single kid _actually_ safer is the bit nobody's rushing to answer.

And remember [last week's Meta AI hack](https://geekout.mattnavarra.com/p/meta-ai-agents-conversations-event-instagram-ai-hacks)? Hackers waltzing into Instagram accounts by asking Meta's support bot nicely, like it was holding the door open for them…

…Well, Meta's now [put a number on it](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/06/09/technology/instagram-hack-ai-bug.html): 20,225 accounts compromised — the Obama White House, US Space Force, Sephora

All handed over by the very same AI Meta wants running your business. 😬

Meta's defence? The tool "worked properly and functioned as intended."

Ah. So it's _meant_ to do that? Reassuring.

Plenty more below.

Let's crack on! 👇

— Matt-

#### 
**A UK teen social ban is back on the cards (and the US isn’t happy)**

UK set to ban under-16s from social media, livestreaming, disappearing messages: (https://www.politico.eu/article/ministers-to-ban-under-16s-from-social-media-livestreaming-disappearing-messages/)

Why are countries around the world rushing to ban social media for under-16s? 

Perhaps because it’s an easy political win in a world of difficult choices, and the only people who will really get upset are too young to vote…? 🤔

So it should be no surprise that a teen social ban is back on the cards in the UK, where the idea had been downplayed for a few months. 

With an uproar about cuts to defence spending, the far-right (endorsed by the world’s first [trillionaire](https://www.reuters.com/business/media-telecom/spacex-ipo-makes-elon-musk-worlds-first-trillionaire-2026-06-11/)) stoking violence at any opportunity, and a leadership challenge brewing, Keir Starmer needs that easy win.

Politico reports that a policies are being rushed through to get announced on Monday:

_“UK ministers will announce a ban on under-16s accessing certain social media platforms as well as livestreaming, disappearing messages, and functionalities that allow communication with adult strangers”_

Oh, and…_ “_**_Social media curfews for 16 and 17 year-olds_**_ could also be implemented but this is still to be confirmed”_.

If this all proves to be true, expect the lobbying machine to go into overdrive on both sides. Social media companies will seek to have their platforms exempted from the rules, and child safety campaigners will push for maximum restrictions.

But in his rush for a win, and a big policy to have to his name if he is replaced soon, Starmer might unleash some unintended consequences. 

This week he [gave](https://www.theguardian.com/technology/2026/jun/08/starmer-tech-firms-ultimatum-block-explicit-images-children-phones?CMP=Share_iOSApp_Other)** **Apple and Google three months to block explicit images on children’s phones. Messaging app company Signal has already spoken out about how the move [won’t protect children](https://www.theregister.com/security/2026/06/09/signal-uks-child-nude-block-threat-wont-protect-children/5252761) and might drive the app to be withdrawn from the UK market.

Meanwhile, a social media ban would enrage the White House, which has already [warned](https://www.theguardian.com/uk-news/2026/jun/09/crackdown-on-tech-platforms-will-go-ahead-despite-us-intervention-says-no-10?CMP=Share_iOSApp_Other) the UK not to place too much of a burden on US tech companies.

And as we’ve seen in Australia, kids find a way around these bans. Just like teenagers smoking behind the bike sheds at school, they’ll still be social online somehow. Will they be more safe just because it’s illegal?

So Starmer might get a few cheers from parents and a smattering of good headlines, but he might not make a huge impact on child safety… all while upsetting everyone from a 13-year-old in Washington, North East England, to a President in Washington, DC.
